narrative: Your law school application was reviewed by an admissions model designed to predict the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, based on patterns from thousands of past students' academic records, test scores, and backgrounds. For you, with your undergraduate GPA of 2.70, LSAT score of 28, middle family income bracket, and full-time study plans, the model estimated a 95.8% chance of failing the bar exam—far too high a risk for admission, leading to the rejection of your application. The decision hinged on just a few key factors from your profile that stood out as strong signals of potential struggle, compared to those who succeeded. Most influential was your Year 3 class rank, placing you in the bottom decile (1st out of 10), while students who passed the bar averaged around the 6th decile—meaning your later academic performance pulled the prediction heavily toward failure, overriding other positives. Next, your Year 1 rank in the 2nd decile similarly weighed against you, as successful bar takers typically started much stronger, around the 6th decile on average. Your LSAT score also contributed to this direction, being notably lower than the 37 average among those who passed, further tipping the scales. These three elements together dominated the model's thinking, painting a picture of inconsistent grades and test performance that didn't align with profiles of admitted students who went on to pass. While your full-time commitment matches the norm for successful applicants (over 93% of them), and your background fits a common middle-income pattern, they weren't enough to counterbalance the academic red flags. In essence, you compared unfavorably to typical accepted students, whose stronger ranks and higher LSATs signaled a smoother path to bar success. This isn't a judgment on your potential or drive—models like this focus narrowly on historical predictors. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score, strengthening your later-year grades through targeted coursework or tutoring, and gathering letters highlighting your resilience and growth. Reapplying after addressing these could shift the odds dramatically in your favor.
